title: Fermented Indulgent Italian Long-bean Ribollita
description: A hearty, indulgent Italian dish featuring fermented long-beans and confit tomatoes, served in a traditional ribollita form.

ingredients.name: Long-beans
ingredients.notes: such as Romano beans or Italian flat beans

instructions: Combine long-beans, confit-tomato, garlic, and red onion in a large bowl, and let it ferment at room temperature for 1-2 days, until it develops a tangy flavor.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the fermented long-bean mixture to the pot and cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the canned cannellini beans, broth, and white wine vinegar to the pot, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 20-25 minutes, or until it thickens slightly.
Season the ribollita with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the ribollita hot, garnished with fresh basil leaves, if desired.
